title: Seismo-turbidites in Aysén Fjord (southern Chile) reveal a complex pattern of interacting seismic sources
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: Seismo-turbidites in Aysén Fjord (45.5°S, southern Chile) are analyzed using sub-bottom profiler, sediment cores, and multibeam bathymetry data to reconstruct the paleoseismic record of the region. The complex pattern of mass transport deposits reveals multiple interacting seismic sources including the megathrust, crustal faults, and volcanic activity. The 2007 Aysén earthquake sequence is identified in the sedimentary record.

location.description: Aysén Fjord (~45.5°S) southern Chile; seismo-turbidite paleoseismic record from sub-bottom profiler, bathymetry, and sediment cores

processing.summary: Sub-bottom profiler, multibeam bathymetry, and sediment cores from Aysén Fjord (~45.5°S) are analyzed to reconstruct the paleoseismic record through seismo-turbidite stratigraphy. Complex interacting seismic sources (megathrust, crustal faults, volcanic) are identified. The 2007 Aysén earthquake sequence is recognized in the sedimentary record.
workflow: 4. Correlation of turbidite layers across cores
processing.notes: JGR paleoseismology paper using indirect marine seismic (sub-bottom) and sediment cores in fjord setting
